commits.messageHeadline: Initial commit with task details for issue #467

commits.messageBody:
Implements live terminal output monitoring for screen sessions in Telegram,
as requested in issue #467. This feature displays real-time terminal output
in a Telegram message that updates automatically until the command completes.

Key features:
- Real-time screen session monitoring with live message updates
- Automatic message freeze when command completes
- Full log file attachment when session ends
- Security: automatically disabled for private repositories
- Rate-limited updates to respect Telegram API limits
- Smart change detection to avoid unnecessary updates

Implementation details:
- Created screen-watcher.lib.mjs module for screen monitoring functionality
- Added --auto-start-screen-watch-message option to telegram-bot CLI
- Integrated watcher into /solve command handler
- Added repository visibility check to prevent use with private repos
- Uses screen hardcopy for efficient output capture
- Formats output as code blocks with truncation for Telegram limits

The option is off by default and only works for public repositories to
prevent potential information leakage.

Inspired by konard/telegram-terminal-bot proof of concept.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.messageHeadline: Revert "Initial commit with task details for issue #467"

commits.messageBody:
This commit merges the latest changes from main branch (0.42.2) into the
screen-watcher feature branch and resolves all conflicts.

## Changes

### Merge Resolution
- Merged origin/main with latest Sentry integration and lenv configuration
- Resolved conflict in src/telegram-bot.mjs by combining both sets of imports
- Added screen-watcher imports alongside Sentry and lenv-reader imports
- Integrated screen watcher into updated configuration system using config object

### Version Bump
- Bumped version from 0.42.2 to 0.42.3 to satisfy CI version check

### Lint Fixes
- Fixed unused variable 'error' in screen-watcher.lib.mjs (changed to catch without parameter)
- Removed unused import 'isScreenSessionRunning' from telegram-bot.mjs
- Removed unused variable 'replyMsg' in telegram-bot.mjs

### Feature Implementation Verified
- ✅ --auto-start-screen-watch-message option properly integrated
- ✅ Async watcher process creates and updates Telegram message
- ✅ Screen session frozen on completion with log file attachment
- ✅ Disabled by default and for private repositories
- ✅ Uses isRepoPrivate() function to check repository visibility

All lint checks pass, ready for CI validation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
